details: Dependency-confusion stub: @veertly/web-app uses a high sentinel version (99.9.9), a scoped name mimicking a real service, and a tiny (1010-byte) tarball. A preinstall hook runs index.js, which collects os.userInfo().username, os.hostname(), and the current working directory, then beacons them to api[.]telegram[.]org/bot8775202566:.../sendMessage with chat_id=1483949647. The error handler is deliberately silent to avoid surfacing failures. Outbound telemetry exfil to an external API at install time. A @veertly-scoped package (version 99.9.9) whose preinstall script (node index.js) collects os.userInfo().username, os.hostname(), and process.cwd() and exfiltrates them to Telegram via api[.]telegram[.]org using a hardcoded bot token and chat ID 1483949647. The script silently swallows errors to evade notice. The description framing it as security research is a pretext for stealthy host reconnaissance. A @veertly-scoped package (version 99.9.9) with a preinstall hook (node index.js) that collects os.userInfo().username, os.hostname(), and process.cwd() and exfiltrates them to Telegram via a hardcoded bot token and chat ID 1483949647. Arabic comments frame it as a research proof-of-concept, but the mechanism is straightforward install-time data exfiltration. A sibling version (100.0.2) carries a different preinstall payload that beacons to 13[.]50[.]241[.]66:8080/poc-Swisscom-Abuelkhair.

cwes.name: Inclusion of Functionality from Untrusted Control Sphere
